metadata.notes: Boundary evals testing dependency lens discipline against Phase 4 fixtures: (1) dependency lens on a completeness fixture (epic-no-decomposition) must not produce findings about the absent Stories list — only uncaptured couplings are in scope; (2) dependency lens on a testability fixture (tautological-ac) must not flag measurability or tautological criteria — only uncaptured couplings. Both evals passed on first run.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='dependency'
No finding body alleges the absent Stories list or lack of child story decomposition
expectations.evidence: Four findings present. All address uncaptured couplings: (1) PostgreSQL RLS and API gateway capability not listed as upstream blockers; (2) infrastructure team constraint not captured; (3) downstream enterprise-prospect tickets not in Blocks; (4) ordering constraints between implied child stories unrecorded. No finding says 'add a Stories list' or 'this epic lacks decomposition'.

expectations.text: Findings address uncaptured couplings (blockers, consumers, cross-team, ordering), not section-level structural gaps
expectations.evidence: Finding 4 references 'no child story list' as context for why ordering constraints cannot be captured, but the finding is about the ordering constraints themselves — not the absent Stories list. The suggestion says 'when child stories are created, record explicit ordering constraints', not 'add a Stories list'.

expectations.text: Output contains a valid JSON code block with lens='dependency'
No finding about measurability, verifiability, or tautological criterion wording
expectations.evidence: Four findings present. All address uncaptured couplings: (1) security audit report not captured as upstream dependency; (2) schema migration DBA/cross-team action not captured; (3) recovery flow communication/ops dependency uncaptured; (4) downstream auth-contract consumers not listed as Blocks. No finding mentions tautological wording or unmeasurable criteria.

expectations.text: Findings address dependency couplings implied by ticket body that are absent from Dependencies section
expectations.evidence: All four findings identify couplings implied by body text (audit-driven requirements, schema migration ownership, recovery channel, downstream consumers) that should appear in Dependencies but don't.

notes: Boundary evals confirm dependency lens stays in its lane when presented with Phase 4 completeness and testability fixtures.
Both evals passed on first run — no SKILL.md iteration required for dependency boundary discipline.

Dependency lens correctly identified rich coupling content in both 'structural gap' fixtures (epic-no-decomposition and tautological-ac), confirming it can find real work on any ticket regardless of which other lens's failure mode the ticket was designed to exhibit.
